quotes.quote_original: ἐν ἀρχῇ ἦν ὁ λόγος · καὶ ὁ λόγος ἦν πρὸς τὸν θεόν · καὶ θεὸς ἦν ὁ λόγος
quotes.quote_english: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: Establishes the divinity of the Logos. The Word is not a lesser emanation but is itself God. Foundation of both orthodox Christology and Gnostic Logos theology.

quotes.quote_original: καὶ ὁ λόγος σὰρξ ἐγένετο
quotes.quote_english: And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: God entered humanity. If divinity can become flesh, the boundary between divine and human is permeable. The orthodox read this as unique to Christ; the Gnostics read it as paradigmatic.

quotes.quote_original: ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν τέκνα θεοῦ γενέσθαι
quotes.quote_english: He gave them the right to become children of God.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: Divine sonship is available to those who receive the Logos. The Gnostics read this as awakening the divine spark; the orthodox read it as adoption by grace.

quotes.quote_original: μονογενὴς θεὸς ὁ ὢν εἰς τὸν κόλπον τοῦ πατρὸς
quotes.quote_english: The only-begotten God, the one who is in the bosom of the Father, he has made him known.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: Sinaiticus reads 'only-begotten God' (monogenes theos), not 'only-begotten Son.' If Christ is the unique God, divinity may be his exclusive property. Key textual variant.

quotes.quote_original: ἐγὼ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἕν ἐσμεν
quotes.quote_english: I and the Father are one.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: The neuter 'one' (hen, not heis) means 'one thing,' not 'one person.' Unity of substance or merely of will? The Arians, Nicenes, and Gnostics all claimed this verse.

quotes.quote_original: ἐγὼ εἶπα θεοί ἐστε
quotes.quote_english: I said, you are gods.
quotes.author: John (attributed), quoting Psalm 82:6
quotes.significance: Jesus quotes scripture calling human beings 'gods.' The most direct claim that divinity is not exclusive. Cited by every major theosis theologian from Clement to Palamas.

quotes.quote_original: ἵνα πάντες ἓν ὦσιν · καθὼς σύ πάτερ ἐν ἐμοὶ κἀγὼ ἐν σοί · ἵνα καὶ αὐτοὶ ἐν ἡμῖν ὦσιν
quotes.quote_english: That they all may be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be in us.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: Christ prays that the mutual indwelling between Father and Son extend to all believers. The most explicit theosis prayer in the New Testament.

quotes.quote_original: ἔσεσθε οὖν ὑμεῖς τέλειοι ὡς ὁ πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ οὐράνιος τέλειός ἐστιν
quotes.quote_english: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.
quotes.author: Matthew (attributed)
quotes.significance: The command to become teleios (complete, having reached the telos). The theosis tradition reads this as an ontological command to become like God, not merely a moral aspiration.

quotes.quote_original: ἵνα διὰ τούτων γένησθε θείας κοινωνοὶ φύσεως
quotes.quote_english: That through them you may become partakers of the divine nature.
quotes.author: Peter (attributed)
quotes.significance: The most explicit theosis statement in the New Testament. Koinonoi physeos = sharers in the nature (ontological, not merely moral). The proof-text for Eastern Orthodox divinization.

quotes.quote_original: Verbum Dei, Dominus noster Iesus Christus, qui propter immensam suam dilectionem factus est quod sumus nos, uti nos perficeret esse quod est ipse.
quotes.quote_english: The Word of God, our Lord Jesus Christ, who through his transcendent love became what we are, that he might bring us to be what he himself is.
quotes.author: Irenaeus of Lyon
quotes.significance: The earliest systematic theosis formula. More radical than Athanasius: not 'God' in the abstract but 'what he himself is.' The verb perficeret (might perfect/complete) implies a process.

quotes.quote_original: Αὐτὸς γὰρ ἐνηνθρώπησεν, ἵνα ἡμεῖς θεοποιηθῶμεν
quotes.quote_english: He became man that we might be made God.
quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.significance: The most famous sentence in the history of the theosis doctrine. The verb theopoiethomen (might be made god) is active and ontological, not metaphorical.

quotes.quote_original: misit scintillam, quae correxit hominem et suscitauit et fecit eum uiuere
quotes.quote_english: He sent a spark, which corrected man and raised him up and made him live.
quotes.author: Filastrius of Brescia (reporting Saturninus)
quotes.significance: The Gnostic divine spark (scintilla): a fragment of divine light sent into the body made by angels. The spark alone is saved; all else is abandoned. The most concise summary of Gnostic soteriology.

quotes.quote_original: Animam ergo solam saluari, corpus autem hominis non saluari arbitratur.
quotes.quote_english: He holds that the soul alone is saved, but that the body of man is not saved.
quotes.author: Filastrius of Brescia (reporting Valentinus)
quotes.significance: The Valentinian position: only the spiritual element in humanity participates in the divine. The body, made by the Demiurge, is excluded. A radical theosis limited to the soul.

quotes.quote_original: panānumma Ūta-napištim amilu / inanna Ūta-napištim u sinništassu lū ēmūū kīma ilīni anaḥnīti
quotes.quote_english: Previously Utnapishtim was a mortal man. Now let Utnapishtim and his wife become like the gods, like us.
quotes.author: Sin-leqi-unninni (attributed)
quotes.source: Epic of Gilgamesh, Tablet XI, lines 241–245
quotes.significance: The Mesopotamian answer: divinity (immortality) was granted once, to one man, and never again. Gilgamesh is refused. The oldest argument that the divine-human boundary is permanent.

quotes.quote_original: σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός · ἐν σοὶ εὐδόκησα
quotes.quote_english: You are my beloved Son; in you I am well pleased.
quotes.author: Mark (attributed)
quotes.significance: The divine voice at the baptism. Addressed to Jesus alone ('You are'), not declared publicly ('This is'). The adoptionists read this as the moment Christ received divinity; the orthodox read it as confirmation of pre-existing sonship.

quotes.quote_english: We are called to become gods, not by nature but by grace.
quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.significance: The distinction between theosis by nature (impossible) and by grace (the orthodox claim). Chrysostom's formulation became standard.

quotes.quote_english: The true gnostic has already become god.
quotes.author: Clement of Alexandria
quotes.significance: Clement reclaims 'gnosis' from the Gnostics: true knowledge does lead to divinization, but through the Church, not secret revelation.

quotes.quote_english: The divine light is not knowledge but surpasses knowledge.
quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.significance: The essence-energies distinction: God's essence is unknowable, but his energies (including the uncreated light) are participable. The final Orthodox synthesis on theosis.

quotes.quote_original: Deus natus homo est, ut homo nasceretur Deus.
quotes.quote_english: God was born as man so that man might be born as God.
quotes.author: Hilary of Poitiers
quotes.significance: The Western Latin tradition affirming theosis in almost identical terms to Athanasius.

quotes.quote_original: Unigenitus Dei Filius, suae divinitatis volens nos esse participes.
quotes.quote_english: The only-begotten Son of God, wanting to make us sharers in his divinity.
quotes.author: Thomas Aquinas
quotes.significance: Even the greatest Western scholastic affirms participation in divinity as the purpose of the Incarnation.

quotes.quote_english: The blood of God.
quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.significance: One of the earliest assertions of Christ's full divinity, written by a bishop on his way to martyrdom. If Christ has 'blood of God,' then divinity entered flesh.

quotes.quote_english: The soul deemed worthy to participate in the Spirit becomes all light, all face, all eye.
quotes.author: Pseudo-Macarius
quotes.significance: The mystical vision of total transformation: not a metaphor but an ontological claim about what happens when the Spirit fills a person.

quotes.quote_english: Anu offered him the bread of life and the water of life, but Adapa refused them.
quotes.author: Anonymous (Babylonian)
quotes.significance: The second Mesopotamian argument against theosis: Adapa had the chance at immortality and was tricked into refusing it. Humanity's exclusion from divinity as cosmic accident, not divine decree.

quotes.quote_original: τὴν αὐτὴν εἰκόνα μεταμορφούμεθα ἀπὸ δόξης εἰς δόξαν
quotes.quote_english: We are being transformed into the same image from glory to glory.
quotes.author: Paul (attributed)
quotes.significance: Theosis as progressive transformation: not instantaneous but a process, moving from one degree of glory to the next, into the image (eikon) of Christ.

quotes.quote_original: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος ὑμεῖς τὰ κλήματα
quotes.quote_english: I am the vine, you are the branches.
quotes.author: John (attributed)
quotes.significance: Organic union with Christ: not merely following a teacher but being a living part of the divine organism. The branches share the life of the vine.

quotes.quote_original: tat tvam asi
quotes.quote_english: You are That.
quotes.author: Upanishadic Sages
quotes.significance: The Eastern theosis: the individual self (atman) IS the universal divine reality (Brahman). Not 'you can become God' but 'you already are.'

quotes.quote_original: aham brahmasmi
quotes.quote_english: I am Brahman.
quotes.author: Upanishadic Sages
quotes.significance: The Self discovers it is identical with the ultimate reality. The most direct theosis claim in any tradition.

quotes.quote_original: ruupam shuunyataa shuunyataiva ruupam
quotes.quote_english: Form is emptiness, emptiness is form.
quotes.author: Heart Sutra
quotes.significance: Buddhism's answer: there is no divine nature to possess because all distinctions (including divine/human) are empty. The dissolution of the question itself.

quotes.quote_english: All beings possess the Tathagata-garbha (Buddha-nature).
quotes.author: Tathagatagarbha Sutra
quotes.significance: The Buddhist divine spark: every being already has the seed of Buddhahood within. Not earned but uncovered.

quotes.quote_original: ana al-haqq
quotes.quote_english: I am the Truth (God).
quotes.author: Mansur al-Hallaj
quotes.significance: The Sufi mystic executed for claiming identity with God. The Islamic parallel to both John 10:30 and Aham Brahmasmi. Al-Hallaj was crucified.

quotes.quote_english: I died as a mineral and became a plant. I died as a plant and became an animal. I died as an animal and became human. What should I fear? When have I become less by dying?
quotes.author: Rumi
quotes.significance: Theosis as evolutionary ascent: each death is a transformation into a higher form. The human is not the endpoint.

quotes.quote_original: phuge monou pros monon
quotes.quote_english: The flight of the alone to the Alone.
quotes.author: Plotinus
quotes.significance: The philosophical foundation of theosis: the soul's return to the One from which it emanated. Not a gift but a homecoming.

quotes.quote_english: The eye through which I see God is the same eye through which God sees me.
quotes.author: Meister Eckhart
quotes.significance: The most radical Western theosis statement. Not two eyes but one. The seer and the seen are identical. Condemned by the Pope.

quotes.quote_original: gefinn Odhni, sjalfr sjalfum mer
quotes.quote_english: Given to Odin, myself to myself.
quotes.author: Anonymous (Norse)
quotes.significance: The Norse god who sacrifices himself to himself for knowledge. The parallel to Christ's crucifixion: divine self-sacrifice as the path to transcendent wisdom.

quotes.quote_original: dao ke dao fei chang dao
quotes.quote_english: The Tao that can be spoken is not the eternal Tao.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu
quotes.significance: The Taoist answer: the divine cannot be named, defined, or possessed. It can only be aligned with. The question itself is the obstacle.

quotes.quote_english: The Son is a creature. There was when he was not.
quotes.author: Arius
quotes.significance: If Christ himself is not fully God by nature, then theosis through Christ is impossible. The Arian position that nearly won.

quotes.quote_original: laqad kafara alladhina qalu inna allaha huwa al-masihu ibnu maryam
quotes.quote_english: They have certainly disbelieved who say, God is the Messiah, son of Mary.
quotes.author: Quran
quotes.significance: The Quranic rejection of divine incarnation. If God did not become human, the entire mechanism of Christian theosis collapses.

quotes.quote_original: laysa kamithlihi shay'un
quotes.quote_english: There is nothing like unto Him.
quotes.author: Quran
quotes.significance: God's absolute transcendence and incomparability. If nothing is like God, then becoming like God is a category error.

quotes.quote_original: finitum non capax infiniti
quotes.quote_english: The finite cannot contain the infinite.
quotes.author: John Calvin
quotes.significance: The Reformed Protestant rejection of theosis: God's nature is absolutely other. Human nature cannot contain or participate in divine nature.

quotes.quote_english: Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth?
quotes.author: Job
quotes.significance: God's rebuke to Job: the gap between Creator and creature is absolute. You cannot question God, let alone become God.

quotes.quote_original: mataiotes mataioteton ta panta mataiotes
quotes.quote_english: Vanity of vanities, all is vanity.
quotes.author: Ecclesiastes
quotes.significance: The anti-theosis of despair: nothing changes, nothing transcends, nothing is new under the sun. Human aspiration to the divine is futile.

quotes.quote_english: If the self were the same as the aggregates, it would be subject to arising and ceasing.
quotes.author: Nagarjuna
quotes.significance: The Buddhist philosophical demolition: there is no permanent self to be divinized. If there is no atman, theosis has no subject.

quotes.quote_english: If cattle and horses had hands, they would draw gods like cattle and horses.
quotes.author: Xenophanes
quotes.significance: The first philosophical critique of anthropomorphic gods. If our gods look like us, that says more about us than about the divine.

quotes.quote_english: The gods live in perfect tranquility, unconcerned with human affairs.
quotes.author: Lucretius
quotes.significance: The Epicurean materialist rejection: the gods exist but don't care about us. There is no divine-human relationship to deepen.

quotes.quote_english: How can a mortal body be God?
quotes.author: Celsus
quotes.significance: The pagan philosophical attack on Christian incarnation: divinity and mortality are incompatible categories.

quotes.quote_original: nāsadāsīn no sadāsīt tadānīṃ
quotes.quote_english: Then there was neither non-being nor being.
quotes.author: Rigveda
quotes.significance: The oldest cosmological skepticism on record. Even the gods came after creation; perhaps no one knows whence it arose.

quotes.quote_original: ἐν ἀρχῇ ἐποίησεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν
quotes.quote_english: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
quotes.author: Moses (attributed)
quotes.significance: Creation ex nihilo as the foundation of Western metaphysics. Time, space, and matter all begin with a single divine act.

quotes.quote_original: enūma eliš lā nabû šamāmū
quotes.quote_english: When on high the heavens had not been named.
quotes.author: Anonymous (Babylonian)
quotes.significance: The Mesopotamian creation epic: the cosmos arose from primordial waters when the gods were named into existence.

quotes.quote_original: 己所不欲勿施於人
quotes.quote_english: What you do not want done to yourself, do not do to others.
quotes.author: Confucius
quotes.significance: The Silver Rule, predating its Christian formulation by 500 years. Ethics derived from empathy rather than divine command.

quotes.quote_original: ἕωθεν προλέγειν ἑαυτῷ συντεύξομαι περιέργῳ ἀχαρίστῳ
quotes.quote_english: Begin the morning by saying to thyself: I shall meet with the busybody, the ungrateful, the arrogant.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius
quotes.significance: The Stoic emperor's daily preparation: anticipate human failure and remain unmoved. The foundation of Stoic ethical practice.

quotes.quote_original: manopubbaṅgamā dhammā manoseṭṭhā manomayā
quotes.quote_english: All that we are is the result of what we have thought.
quotes.author: Buddha
quotes.significance: Mind as the origin of all states. The first verse of the Buddha's most quoted text: ethics begins in thought, not action.

quotes.quote_original: deyr fé deyja frændr deyr sjalfr it sama en orðstírr deyr aldregi
quotes.quote_english: Cattle die, kinsmen die, you yourself shall die. But the fame of one who has done well never dies.
quotes.author: Anonymous (Norse)
quotes.significance: Norse pagan immortality is not theosis but reputation. Glory outlives the body where the soul does not.

quotes.quote_original: οἶδα οὐδὲν εἰδώς
quotes.quote_english: I know that I know nothing.
quotes.author: Socrates (via Plato)
quotes.significance: The foundation of philosophical humility. Wisdom begins with the recognition of ignorance.

quotes.quote_original: πάντες ἄνθρωποι τοῦ εἰδέναι ὀρέγονται φύσει
quotes.quote_english: All men by nature desire to know.
quotes.author: Aristotle
quotes.significance: The opening line of the Metaphysics: knowledge is not optional but constitutive of human nature.

quotes.quote_original: hiraṇmayena pātreṇa satyasyāpihitaṃ mukham
quotes.quote_english: The face of truth is hidden by a golden disk.
quotes.author: Upanishadic Sages
quotes.significance: Truth is veiled even by what shines. Mystical knowledge requires the removal of brightness, not its addition.

quotes.quote_original: ἀθάνατον ἄρα ψυχή
quotes.quote_english: The soul is immortal.
quotes.author: Plato
quotes.significance: The first sustained philosophical defense of immortality in Western thought. The soul cannot die because it shares in life itself.

quotes.quote_english: I have not done evil to mankind. I have not blasphemed a god.
quotes.author: Anonymous (Egyptian)
quotes.source: Book of the Dead, Spell 125 (Negative Confession)
quotes.significance: The Egyptian heart-weighing: salvation through ethical declaration. The earliest extant moral self-examination in afterlife judgment.

quotes.quote_english: O nobly born, the time has come for you to seek the Path.
quotes.author: Padmasambhava (attributed)
quotes.significance: The Tibetan Book of the Dead's instruction to the dying: liberation is possible at the moment of death if the mind recognizes its true nature.

quotes.quote_original: ποῦ σου θάνατε τὸ νῖκος
quotes.quote_english: O death, where is thy victory?
quotes.author: Paul (attributed)
quotes.significance: Paul's triumphal taunt to death. The Christian claim that resurrection has emptied the grave of its terror.

quotes.quote_original: ἢ οἱ φιλόσοφοι βασιλεύσωσιν ἐν ταῖς πόλεσιν
quotes.quote_english: Until philosophers rule as kings in cities.
quotes.author: Plato
quotes.significance: The philosopher-king ideal: a society can only be just when wisdom and power are united in the same person.

quotes.quote_original: 太上下知有之
quotes.quote_english: The best ruler is one whose existence the people merely know.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu
quotes.significance: The Taoist political ideal: minimal interference, invisible rule. Authority is best when it goes unnoticed.

quotes.quote_original: οὐ γὰρ ἔστιν ἐξουσία εἰ μὴ ὑπὸ θεοῦ
quotes.quote_english: There is no authority except from God.
quotes.author: Paul (attributed)
quotes.significance: The doctrine of divinely ordained authority. Used to justify obedience to rulers from Constantine to the modern state.

quotes.quote_original: dukkhaṃ ariyasaccaṃ
quotes.quote_english: This is the noble truth of suffering.
quotes.author: Buddha
quotes.significance: The First Noble Truth: existence is marked by dukkha. The diagnosis from which all Buddhist practice follows.

quotes.quote_english: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him.
quotes.author: Job
quotes.significance: The paradigm of fidelity through undeserved suffering. Trust that survives all evidence against it.

quotes.quote_original: νυνὶ δὲ μένει πίστις ἐλπὶς ἀγάπη
quotes.quote_english: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
quotes.author: Paul (attributed)
quotes.significance: The supremacy of agape: not affection or desire, but self-giving love. Paul ranks it above faith itself.

quotes.quote_original: بشنو این نی چون شکایت می‌کند
quotes.quote_english: Listen to the reed, how it complains, telling the tale of separations.
quotes.author: Rumi
quotes.significance: The opening of the Masnavi: the soul cut from its source longs to return. Love as the wound of separation.

quotes.quote_original: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
quotes.quote_english: You have a right to action, but never to its fruits.
quotes.author: Krishna (in Bhagavad Gita)
quotes.significance: The doctrine of nishkama karma: act without attachment to results. The Hindu answer to the problem of motive.
